messages: I'm adopting a dog next month: an adult male border terrier, 2.5 years old; the rescue set the pickup for March 18, 2026 at 11:00 AM.
They said he's neutered, current on shots, weighs 27 pounds, and stands approximately 15 inches at the shoulder.

My lease only allows one pet and caps the weight at 30 pounds; I also have to sign a pet addendum with a $200 refundable deposit and pay $25 monthly pet rent. The building elevator interior measures 34 inches wide by 54 inches deep.

I booked a new-patient registration at Maplewood Veterinary Clinic, 742 Elm Street (about 0.8 miles away). They require the animal's microchip ID to register—the rescue gave me his microchip, 512743988—and the adoption fee was $95.
I already have an indoor-only six-year-old female tabby cat that is spayed and has never been around dogs, so introductions will need to be slow.
I'm torn between buying a soft-sided travel carrier and a rigid plastic crate for the drive home—what are the practical pros and cons for an adult dog?
How soon should I bring the new dog's toys and bedding into shared spaces, or is it better to keep them confined to a single room at first?
What's a solid starter shopping list for a new dog owner on a modest budget—leash, collar, food, bowls, bed, crate, first-aid basics?
Has anyone had success with clicker training for basic manners, or is positive reinforcement without the clicker just as effective?
Any advice for choosing a kibble that balances quality and price without getting lost in brand marketing claims?
Does pet insurance tend to pay off for young adult dogs, or is it usually more worthwhile for very young or much older animals?
How long are typical group obedience classes, and what's a reasonable weekly time commitment for someone working full days?
What's the best way to find a trustworthy dog walker or sitter—do formal background checks matter, and what's a usual price range?
Do you have low-cost enrichment ideas for an adult dog that needs mental stimulation but not excessive physical exertion?
When it comes to dental care, are special tools necessary or will twice-weekly brushing with pet toothpaste suffice?
Any tips to keep a small living space smelling fresh with a dog indoors for most of the day?
What are common red flags to watch for in adoption paperwork—anything that should make me pause before signing?
For a dog that occasionally pulls on walks, which harness styles tend to be the most effective and humane?
Should I book a groomer appointment shortly after adoption or wait until the dog settles in a bit?
How can I scout safe, well-maintained off-leash areas near my city without relying solely on user reviews?
Would a single professional behavior consult be worth it if the dog shows mild reactivity, or are multiple sessions generally required?
What's your go-to strategy for pet-proofing electrical cords and removing toxic plants from the main living area?
For socialization, is one-on-one meetups with calm, vaccinated dogs preferable to group play sessions for newcomers?
Any tips for smoothly transitioning a dog from shelter kibble to a new brand without digestive upset?
For custom name tags, aside from phone number, what information would be essential to include?
Which smartphone apps are genuinely helpful for new dog owners—training trackers, medication reminders, or appointment scheduling?
What's a realistic estimate for expenses during the first three months of adoption, including supplies, training, and routine care?
Do you recommend waiting before enrolling in dog sports or activity classes to let the dog settle, or is early involvement beneficial?
Any practical strategies for preventing or easing separation anxiety in dogs that are newly moved into an apartment?
Do adult dogs adapt quicker to a new home than puppies, and are there useful timelines to expect behavior normalization?
Which toys tend to survive heavy chewers—are there brands or materials reliably more durable?
How transparent are histories typically when adopting an adult dog—should I expect clear prior-owner records or be prepared for gaps?

I bought a collapsible soft-sided travel crate that measures 36 by 24 by 24 inches—do you think carrying the dog plus this crate from the pickup point up to my unit in a single trip will be practical given my building's limited access?

If I go straight to my usual clinic after collecting the dog, will their online new-patient form accept the implant identifier the rescue provided, or should I bring printed documentation to avoid delays?
Since there's already an older indoor-only cat at home who hasn't met dogs before, what step-by-step introduction would minimize stress for both animals in the first week?
The rescue asked for payment at pickup—should I expect they accept card, or would it be wise to bring cash or mobile payment as backup?
My lease required a refundable pet deposit plus a modest monthly pet charge—would asking the landlord for an explicit written addendum be necessary, or is the standard deposit usually enough?

I scheduled the handoff for a mid-morning slot on that specific date—do you think there's enough time that same afternoon to fit in the brief registration at my nearby clinic, or should I plan the vet visit for another day?
I ordered a medium harness listed up to 13.5 kg—based on the dog's build, should I exchange it for a larger size or is medium likely to fit?
The crate's internal height is 24 inches—would that be sufficient for the dog to stand comfortably, or is it better to look for a taller option?
